title: Graceful embedder degradation: FTS fallback on model load failure
context: The availability probe (createRequire.resolve) can succeed while runtime ESM import or model load fails—e.g., package only resolvable via NODE_PATH or missing native backend. Previously this crashed with a stack trace.
decision: Wrap embedAll in try/catch to gracefully degrade to keyword search (FTS) with actionable error message instead of crashing. Pinned dtype to fp32 to silence stderr noise and guarantee vector consistency with persisted data.
consequences: System stays operational in degraded mode when semantic model unavailable—queries work via keyword search
User sees diagnostic hint (install scope mismatch) instead of stack trace
Requires README clarification: transformers must be installed in the same scope as hunch (global→global, source→repo node_modules)
Consistent failure mode across embed and query paths (both degrade to FTS)
alternatives_rejected: Crash with full stack trace (poor UX, hard to diagnose for users)
Fail fast at initialization (blocks all queries, not just semantic)
Silent fallback without notification (user confused about missing semantic search)
